_description: Claude-consumed JSON registry for the frontmatter pillar. Loaded by pre-write-guard.sh at runtime and read by the librarian frontmatter-coverage-audit. Carries R-32, R-33, R-37, R-39, R-40, R-41 plus the type registry, tier compliance, archetype-conditional fields, path-routing rules, and the archetype enum. Frontmatter is the API every file exposes to the system; this pillar declares the fields, per-type contracts, AND the rules that enforce them. SP13 T-4 dissolved schemas/vault-schema.json into this pillar (types + tier assignment + path routing + archetype binding) plus governance/tagging-rules.json (tag-prefix taxonomy). See research/vault-construction/frontmatter-design.md for the narrative rationale.

_design_notes.consumer: pre-write-guard.sh — SCHEMA_KEY case statement + R-32 type-allowlist DENY + R-33 placement advisory; post-write-verify.sh — R-39 provides-presence advisory + R-41 archetype-field-compliance audit; librarian frontmatter-coverage-audit (R-39, R-40, R-41)
absorbed_from_vault_schema: types (21 per-type entries; were top-level type keys in vault-schema.json)
path_routing (was vault-schema.json `_path_rules`; renamed for clarity per SP13 spec)
archetype_enum (was implicit in vault-schema.json; now explicit declaration)
_design_notes.lockstep_pair: System Governance - Frontmatter.md (narrative spoke) + pre-write-guard.sh (SCHEMA_KEY case) + post-write-verify.sh (type_map)
_design_notes.evolution_protocol: R-37 atomic lockstep — frontmatter-rules.json + System Governance - Frontmatter.md + pre-write-guard.sh SCHEMA_KEY case + post-write-verify.sh type_map updated in one commit. NOTE: schemas/vault-schema.json was DISSOLVED in SP13 T-4 (2026-05-14) into this pillar + governance/tagging-rules.json; the file remains physically present as a frozen artifact carrying a `_dissolved: true` marker until the last consumer migrates (T-3 hooks, T-8 install, T-9 tests, T-10 docs) — no new authoring against the dissolved file.

tier_compliance._description: Canonical three-tier compliance definitions (SP13 T-4: absorbed from dissolved vault-schema.json `tiers` block; this pillar is now the single source of truth for tier semantics + enforcement metadata).

strict.description: System files (scaffold-emitted, /ingest-routed, scraper-aggregated to Inbox/). Hard fail at write-time (R-32 Tier 2 DENY).
strict.applies_to: type entries with tier=strict in this pillar's `types` block

standard.description: User-authored vault content. Soft warning; librarian flags drift via tag-coverage-audit + frontmatter-coverage-audit capabilities.
standard.applies_to: type entries with tier=standard in this pillar's `types` block

minimal.description: Explicit opt-out files. No validation; flagged 'outside system' by librarian. Used for legacy imports, paste-buffer scratch, archives outside lifecycle.
minimal.applies_to: files matching minimal_tier_exemptions glob patterns OR carrying explicit `tier: minimal` frontmatter directive

archetype_enum._description: Canonical registered archetypes (SP13 T-4: absorbed from vault-schema._archetype_enum; was implicit reference target from R-51). Adopters extend via Layer 3 overlay-master.json archetype_extensions per R-52 collision-tiebreaker.

archetype_conditional_fields._description: Fields that may appear in any type entry's required/optional list. Per-type entries declare which apply (the 'per-archetype entries' in the unified model). Adopters extend this list via Layer 3 overlay-master.json; the 14 entries below are the foundation-repo seed. (SP13 T-4: absorbed from vault-schema._archetype_conditional_fields.)

packet_only_fields._description: Fields valid only on entries with type=packet (system-altitude research context packets at claude-stem/research/vault-construction/). Engagement/topic/initiative altitude packets at the user vault use the same shape via the `packet` type entry below. (SP13 T-4: absorbed from vault-schema._packet_only_fields.)

parent_plan.reason: Plan-tree-only field; lives in plans-schema.json + plan-manifest-schema.json, not vault content governance.
parent_plan.disposition: Deferred from v1. Re-visit when planning methodology generalizes for adopters.
parent_plan.do_not: Do NOT add `parent_plan` to any type entry in this pillar. Plan-tree files validate against plans-schema.json + plan-manifest-schema.json separately.

retired_types._description: Types removed from the active type registry; preserved for write-time DENY messaging (R-32 retired_types_handling). (SP13 T-4 2026-05-14: absorbed from vault-schema._retired_types. SP13 Phase 1c 2026-05-14: replacement text abstracted off user-vocabulary folder names per canonical §G; concrete adopter folder patterns live in overlay-master.frontmatter.path_routing.rules[].)

engagement.reason: Type information lives at file level only — folders don't have frontmatter. `engagement` is NOT a canonical TYPE; it is a FIELD slot that propagates folder lineage to files. Folders own structural artifact; field + tag propagate to file level.
engagement.replacement: Use `engagement: <slug>` as FIELD on any file living under the adopter's engagement-archetype lineage folder. Foundation declares the field; adopter's overlay-master.frontmatter.path_routing.rules[] declares the lineage glob (consultant archetype seed or extension). For the engagement-level navigation file itself, use type=`navigation`.

project.reason: Same as `engagement` — folders own project lineage; FIELD `project` propagates lineage to files.
project.replacement: Use `project: <slug>` as FIELD on any file living under the adopter's project sub-cluster lineage folder. Foundation declares the field; adopter's overlay-master.frontmatter.path_routing.rules[] declares the lineage glob. For the project-level overview file itself, use type=`context` or type=`navigation` (per file purpose).

path_routing._description: Cross-cutting folder-lineage validation rules (SP13 T-4 2026-05-14: absorbed from vault-schema._path_rules; renamed for clarity). Hook contract: when a file is written, evaluate matching path rules; if pattern matches, the file MUST satisfy the requires_fields + requires_tags clauses. SP13 Phase 1c 2026-05-14: foundation ships an EMPTY rules[] array per canonical §G+§H — archetype-specific folder patterns belong to adopter overlay-master.frontmatter.path_routing.rules[], not foundation governance. Onboarding wizard (successor sub-plan scope) offers archetype seeds at install time and writes the adopter's selection into overlay-master. Foundation declares the SHAPE (rules[] array contract + per-rule field semantics via _rule_shape_contract below); adopter provides the CONTENT.

_rule_shape_contract._description: Per-rule object shape that adopter overlay-master.frontmatter.path_routing.rules[] entries MUST conform to. Reference contract for the wizard (onboarding successor) when authoring archetype seeds into overlay-master at install time.
_rule_shape_contract.pattern: <glob path with {placeholder} segments for field-bound depth markers>
exemptions: <file-path patterns that bypass this rule (e.g., folder-level navigation/index files)>

path_routing._onboarding_seed_location_pending: onboarding/scaffold/archetype-seeds/<archetype>.json (deferred to onboarding successor sub-plan; SP13 stays scoped to foundation governance abstraction per Session 5 Phase 1c lock 2026-05-14)

types._description: Canonical type registry — 21 active type entries plus retired_types reference. Each entry declares tier + universal_required + per-type required + optional + conditional_required + (optional) body_structure_contract. (SP13 T-4: absorbed from vault-schema.json top-level type keys; this pillar is the single source of truth.)

parent_folder.condition_semantics: depth is the number of path segments between the vault root and the file; depth 1 = `<cluster>/_index.md` (parent_folder omitted because the parent IS the vault root); depth >= 2 = `<cluster>/<instance>/_index.md` or deeper (parent_folder mandatory).
parent_folder.rationale: Navigation-side lineage pointer auto-populated by post-write-verify auto-bootstrap; librarian index-maintain audits for path-vs-frontmatter drift. Distinct from the content-side folder-lineage convention (`engagement:`/`project:`) which uses path_routing. See ../research/vault-construction/_index.md-design.md §parent_folder.

index.body_structure_contract_rationale: Body-structure rules (H1, folder-context paragraph, contents-enum table with column shape + sentinel markers + ordering convention) live in the per-file-type contract following the k8s ValidatingAdmissionPolicy paramKind pattern (matcher in pillar/schema; parameters in separate replaceable resource). See ../research/vault-construction/_index.md-design.md + ../target-state/_index.md-design/structural-requirements.md.

packet._description: Research context packets at all four altitudes (system / engagement / topic / initiative). System-altitude packets canonical at `claude-stem/research/vault-construction/`; engagement/topic/initiative at user vault. Surfaces 180-day `last_reviewed` cadence (system) / 90-day (topic) / continuous (engagement) / closed-at-plan-close (initiative).

archetype-template._description: Scaffold templates at `onboarding/templates/<archetype>/` consumed by install. Pre-populated frontmatter; R-32 100% conformant. Anti-drift principle: archetype templates ship pre-populated upfront; user-added files inherit defaults via templates.

rules.source: ENFORCEMENT-MAP.md R-32 + ../research/vault-construction/frontmatter-design.md §The 3 compliance tiers in detail + ../docs/decisions/0001-tiered-compliance.md
enforcement_layer: governance/frontmatter-rules.json `types` (type allowlist + per-type required/optional field declarations)
rules.failure_mode: Files written with an unknown `type:` value or with missing Strict-tier required fields drift the schema contract silently. Downstream consumers (auto-routing, librarian, scaffold) cannot reason about the file because the contract has been violated. Without write-time DENY, the schema becomes documentation rather than enforcement — production-scale untagged-file backlogs (~500 files observed in a reference deployment) accumulate before audit catches up. The Tier 2 DENY closes the gap at the moment of capture, returning a structured error that enumerates the missing fields and points to the schema entry.
rules.rule_text: Every Strict-tier vault write MUST declare a `type:` value drawn from the governance/frontmatter-rules.json `types` allowlist (21 canonical type entries; 2 retired types preserved in `retired_types`). For Strict-tier files, the universal required fields (`type`, `tags`, `updated`) AND the per-type `required` field list MUST all be present in the YAML frontmatter. Pre-write-guard DENIES writes failing either check; the DENY message enumerates the missing fields plus the pillar entry path. Adding a new type or extending a required-field list requires R-37 atomic lockstep updating this pillar + the narrative spoke + pre-write-guard SCHEMA_KEY case + post-write-verify type_map in one commit. Standard-tier writes emit a warning instead of DENY (operator triages via librarian frontmatter-coverage-audit); Minimal-tier writes proceed without validation.
rules.retired_types_handling: Files declaring a retired `type:` value (currently `engagement`, `project` — retired per ADR-0003) are DENIED with the retirement reference and `replacement` guidance pointing the author to the folder-lineage convention (field-level propagation via `engagement:` + `project:` frontmatter fields plus matching tags). See `retired_types` block.

enforcement_layer: pre-write-guard.sh folder-placement advisory branch (Tier 1; never blocks)
rules.failure_mode: Files written outside the canonical folder for their declared `type:` produce structural drift that compounds — the file is invisible to type-scoped queries, lives in a folder that does not match its archetype, and creates the impression that 'this type lives anywhere.' Once the precedent lands, follow-on writes drift further. The Tier 1 advisory fires at write-time rather than waiting for weekly audit so the operator sees the misplacement at the moment of capture and can move the file before publishing wikilinks.
rules.rule_text: Files declaring a `type:` value SHOULD reside under the canonical folder for that type per governance/frontmatter-rules.json `path_routing` (the per-archetype lineage rules). Pre-write-guard emits a Tier 1 advisory `[R-33 FOLDER PLACEMENT]` when the file's path does not match the type's expected folder pattern. The advisory never blocks — engagement-specific exceptions, cross-engagement reference docs, and adopter-customized folder structures are legitimate causes for divergence. The advisory text enumerates the expected folder pattern and the current path so the author can decide. Persistent advisory firings on the same type/folder pair surface as `placement-drift` findings in the librarian audit.

rules.source: ENFORCEMENT-MAP.md R-37 (cross-cutting meta-rule; canonical entry in governance/_index.json `cross_cutting_meta_rules[]`) + ../research/vault-construction/frontmatter-design.md §R-37 atomic-lockstep protocol + ../research/vault-construction/enforcement-map-design.md §R-37 atomic-lockstep protocol + ../docs/decisions/0005-two-surface-governance-dual-pattern.md
enforcement_layer: pre-write-guard.sh atomic-lockstep branch (DENY on partial-surface writes)
rules.failure_mode: Schema-touching changes that land in one surface without the others produce silent drift — the pillar declares a new type the hook does not recognize; the narrative spoke documents fields the pillar has not added; the rule registry references a field name the pillar spells differently. Within weeks the surfaces diverge enough that no single artifact is authoritative. Drift is detectable but already-broken when the audit surfaces it. R-37 closes the gap at write-time by treating partial-surface commits as the structural error they are.
rules.rule_text: The frontmatter pillar binds three coupled surfaces under R-37 atomic lockstep: (1) governance/frontmatter-rules.json (canonical pillar declaring types, tiers, archetype enum, archetype-conditional fields, path-routing rules, and rule registry; SP13 T-4 absorbed the former schemas/vault-schema.json), (2) onboarding/scaffold/vault-architecture/System Governance - Frontmatter.md (narrative spoke), (3) hooks/pre-write-guard.sh SCHEMA_KEY case-statement + hooks/post-write-verify.sh type_map (runtime mirror). Adding, modifying, or retiring a `type:` value, changing a required-field list, introducing a new field class, or updating the tier-compliance contract requires touching ALL THREE surfaces in a single commit. Pre-write-guard's atomic-lockstep branch DENIES writes that touch one surface without the others, with the missing surfaces enumerated. This entry is the pillar-scoped instantiation of the canonical R-37 meta-rule at governance/_index.json `cross_cutting_meta_rules[]`. The interim coupled-surfaces declaration in governance/_index.json carries hooks/config/gate-config.json (until T-6 retirement) and governance/foundation-master.json (forward) per SP13 retirement-order discipline.
coupled_surfaces: governance/foundation-master.json (forward; built from this pillar via tools/build-foundation-master.sh)

enforcement_layer: post-write-verify.sh provides-presence advisory (Tier 1; combined with R-38 blockquote-summary block)
rules.failure_mode: Long-form files (>200 lines) without a `provides:` declaration become opaque to consumers that route by capability rather than by path. The router cannot determine what concepts the file is canonical for; the librarian cannot detect provides-canonicality drift (R-40) because there is no claim to compare against; downstream agents loading the file have no fast way to decide whether the content is relevant. The advisory surfaces the omission at write-time so the author either declares `provides:` deliberately or accepts the file as content-only.
rules.rule_text: Files exceeding 200 lines SHOULD declare a `provides:` array in their YAML frontmatter enumerating the concepts, rules, or capabilities the file is the canonical source for. Post-write-verify emits a Tier 1 advisory `[R-39 PROVIDES PRESENCE]` when a file over the line threshold lacks `provides:` or has `provides: []`. The advisory never blocks — content-only long-form files (raw transcripts, archive imports, daily-note dumps) are legitimate and the author can ignore the advisory. The librarian frontmatter-coverage-audit tracks recurring R-39 firings as `provides-coverage-drift` findings so the operator can triage at session-close.
exemptions: Archive/** — archived content; provides-declaration is structurally unnecessary
Daily/** — daily notes are temporally-scoped, not capability-scoped
Logs/** — machine-emitted log surfaces; lifecycle is closed at write-time
Inbox/** — aggregation surfaces; provides lives at the canonical destination, not the staging copy

enforcement_layer: drift-allowlist override at hooks/config/drift-allowlist.json (`provides_overlap[]` for deliberate co-canonical pairs)
rules.failure_mode: Two or more canonical-scope files claiming the same `provides:` capability fragment authority. A consumer routing to the capability does not know which file to read; updates to one canonical do not propagate to the other; the two files drift into mutually-contradictory statements within months. Without an audit, the drift is invisible — `grep provides:` returns both files and the consumer picks one by accident. The librarian-audit finding surfaces the collision and asks the operator to either consolidate, archive one source, or register the overlap as deliberate in the drift-allowlist.
rules.rule_text: Within the canonical-scope of the vault (vault root depth-1 + System Governance/** + Skills/**), no two files SHOULD declare the same capability in their `provides:` array. The librarian frontmatter-coverage-audit walks every Standard- and Strict-tier file's `provides:` declaration, builds an inverted index, and emits a `provides-canonicality-drift` finding whenever a capability is claimed by 2+ canonical-scope owners. Severity is `blocking` when any owner sits at vault root or inside System Governance/**; `warning` otherwise. Engagement reference docs, meeting notes, project context files, and daily notes are EXCLUDED from canonical-scope — `provides:` is project-scoped vocabulary there, not vault-wide canonical. Deliberate co-canonical pairs (e.g., a research packet plus its consumer-rendered spoke) register at hooks/config/drift-allowlist.json `provides_overlap[].capability` with rationale + audit-trail entry; allowlist entries mirror into librarian-manifest.json on every run and suppress the finding.
scope_exemptions: Meetings/** — meeting `provides:` is cross-reference helper, not canonical claim (foundation-shipped folder per canonical §F)
Daily/** — daily notes are temporally-scoped (foundation-shipped folder per canonical §F)

_exemption_classes._description: SP13 Phase 1c 2026-05-14: foundation declares the EXEMPTION CLASS shape per canonical §G+§H; concrete adopter folder names (engagement-scoped subfolders, project-scoped subfolders, archetype-cluster subfolders, etc.) live in overlay-master.frontmatter.canonical_scope_exemptions[]. Foundation governance stays archetype-agnostic.

classes.name: instance-scoped
classes.rationale: files inside an archetype-cluster instance (engagement / topic / repo / etc.) are scoped to that instance, not vault-wide canonical

classes.name: sub-cluster-scoped
classes.rationale: files inside an archetype's sub-cluster (project / study / epic / etc.) carry sub-cluster-scoped `provides:` vocabulary, not vault-wide canonical

rules.source: ../research/vault-construction/vault-construction-principles.md commitment 4 (multi-archetype union) + ../research/vault-construction/frontmatter-design.md §Archetype-conditional fields + spec §Archetype-binding rule contract (sibling to R-51 in governance/tagging-rules.json)
enforcement_layer: librarian archetype-consistency audit (audit-time; per-archetype field-coverage verification)
post-write-verify.sh archetype-field advisory (Tier 1; warning on incomplete archetype field-set)
rules.failure_mode: Files declaring an archetype (via `archetype:` frontmatter field or via per-type entry with archetype binding) but missing required archetype-conditional fields fragment the multi-archetype union model. A consultant archetype file missing `engagement:`/`project:` cannot be filtered to engagement-scoped views. A researcher archetype file missing `topic:`/`study:` cannot resolve to the topic taxonomy. The renamed-dimension overlay (developer's `#repo/*` mapping to the consultant's `#engagement/*` structural slot) breaks at consumption time. The audit closes the gap by walking every archetype-declaring file and verifying the required field set per archetype is fully populated.
rules.rule_text: Files declaring an archetype affiliation (either via the `archetype:` frontmatter field or via a `type:` entry with archetype binding declared in governance/frontmatter-rules.json) MUST carry the full required-field set for that archetype per governance/frontmatter-rules.json `archetype_conditional_fields[<archetype>]` declaration. The librarian archetype-consistency audit walks every archetype-declaring file, computes the per-archetype required-field intersection, and emits `archetype-field-compliance-drift` findings when any required field is missing. Severity is `warning` (Tier 1 advisory) — the file lands; the audit surfaces drift at session-close. Adopters extending the archetype enum via Layer 3 overlay-master.json must extend `archetype_conditional_fields` in the same overlay; otherwise the audit reports the unknown archetype as `archetype-not-in-enum` (R-51 sibling at tagging-rules.json carries the write-time DENY for archetype enum violations). The pair (R-41 advisory at audit-time + R-51 DENY at write-time) is the field-side and tag-side enforcement of the multi-archetype binding contract.
rules.sibling_rule: R-51 (governance/tagging-rules.json) — archetype-binding DENY at write-time on `archetype:` field value
exemptions: Files declaring `tier: minimal` — opt-out from validation
Archive/** — closed lifecycle; archetype-completeness is preserved as a historical record, not a live audit target
Files without any archetype declaration — pre-archetype-era content remains in Standard tier without binding
